issuesFound.title: **Form control missing accessible label**
issuesFound.explanation: Several form controls lack accessible labels. Specifically, `<input>` and `<select>` elements are missing `<label for="...">`, `aria-label`, or `aria-labelledby`.
issuesFound.fix:
Add labels to each form control. Example:
  Line 10: `<input id="search-input" type="search" name="q" placeholder="Search docs, blog, tools…" autocomplete="off" aria-label="Search Luminary Studio">` (already has `aria-label`)
  Line 11: `<input id="contact-first-name" type="text" name="first_name" autocomplete="given-name" required aria-required="true">` (missing label)
  Line 12: `<input id="contact-last-name" type="text" name="last_name" autocomplete="family-name" required aria-required="true">` (missing label)
  Line 13: `<input id="contact-email" type="email" name="email" autocomplete="work email" required aria-required="true">` (missing label)
  Line 14: `<input id="contact-company" type="text" name="company" autocomplete="organization">` (missing label)
  Line 15: `<select id="contact-team-size" name="team_size">` (missing label)
  Line 16: `<input id="newsletter-email" type="email" name="email" autocomplete="email" required aria-required="true">` (missing label)
  Line 17: `<input id="newsletter-consent" type="checkbox" name="consent" required aria-required="true">` (not a `<input>` element, skip)

  Corrected form controls:
  Line 11: `<label for="contact-first-name">First name</label>`
  Line 12: `<label for="contact-last-name">Last name</label>`
  Line 13: `<label for="contact-email">Work email address</label>`
  Line 14: `<label for="contact-company">Company name</label>`
  Line 15: `<label for="contact-team-size">Team size</label>`
  Line 16: `<label for="newsletter-email">Your email address</label>`

issuesFound.title: **Nav landmark missing label**
issuesFound.explanation: There are two `<nav>` elements (`main-nav` and `Account navigation`) without a distinguishing label (`aria-label` or `aria-labelledby`). This violates WCAG 2.4.7 (Focus Order) and makes navigation landmarks indistinguishable to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `aria-label` to the second `<nav>` element. Example:
  Line 10: `<nav id="main-nav" aria-label="Main navigation">` (already has `aria-label`)
  Line 11: `<nav aria-label="Account navigation">` (missing `aria-label`)
  Corrected `<nav>`:
  Line 11: `<nav aria-label="Account navigation">`

issuesFound.title: **Missing autocomplete attribute for personal data input**
issuesFound.explanation: The `<input>` elements `contact-first-name`, `contact-last-name`, `contact-email`, and `newsletter-email` suggest personal data collection (given-name, family-name, email) but lack the `autocomplete` attribute, violating WCAG 1.3.5 (Automatic Data Capture).
issuesFound.fix:
Add `autocomplete` attribute to each personal data input. Example:
  Line 10: `<input id="contact-first-name" type="text" name="first_name" autocomplete="given-name" required aria-required="true">` (already has `autocomplete`)
  Line 11: `<input id="contact-last-name" type="text" name="last_name" autocomplete="family-name" required aria-required="true">` (already has `autocomplete`)
  Line 12: `<input id="contact-email" type="email" name="email" autocomplete="work email" required aria-required="true">` (already has `autocomplete`)
  Line 13: `<input id="newsletter-email" type="email" name="email" autocomplete="email" required aria-required="true">` (already has `autocomplete`)

  **Corrected inputs:**
  Line 10: `<input id="contact-first-name" type="text" name="first_name" autocomplete="given-name" required aria-required="true">` (already correct)
  Line 11: `<input id="contact-last-name" type="text" name="last_name" autocomplete="family-name" required aria-required="true">` (already correct)
  Line 12: `<input id="contact-email" type="email" name="email" autocomplete="work email" required aria-required="true">` (already correct)

How Luminary Studio works

Get from installation to your first audit in under five minutes.

  1. 1
    Connect your project

    Install the Luminary CLI (npm install -g @luminary/cli), connect your GitHub or GitLab repository, and point the scanner at your staging URL. Supports single-page apps, server-rendered pages, and native web components.

  2. 2

    Run your first scan

    Execute luminary scan or trigger the scan from inside the VS Code extension. The engine crawls your app, runs 320+ automated checks across all WCAG 2.2 success criteria, and delivers results in real time.

  3. 3

    Review and remediate

    Open the Luminary dashboard to see issues grouped by severity, WCAG criterion, component, and route. Each issue includes a code diff, video walkthrough, and a suggested ARIA or HTML fix.

  4. 4

    Ship and monitor

    Merge the fixes and push to production. Luminary's nightly monitor checks that issues stay resolved, alerts on regressions, and tracks your accessibility score over time so stakeholders always have an up-to-date picture.

What our customers say

Frequently asked questions

Luminary Studio covers WCAG 2.0, 2.1, and 2.2 at levels A, AA, and AAA. We also include checks for EN 301 549 (European standard), Section 508 (US Federal), and the upcoming WCAG 3.0 draft criteria. Our rule set is updated within 30 days of any official standard revision.

Yes. The scanner uses a headless browser to execute JavaScript and test the fully rendered DOM, so it works with React, Vue, Angular, Svelte, Next.js, Remix, and any other JavaScript framework. The VS Code extension also analyses source files statically before the browser even renders them.

You can provide session cookies, OAuth tokens, or a login script that the scanner will execute before crawling. We support cookie-, form-, and token-based authentication. Enterprise plans support SAML and SSO-gated environments.

Luminary only stores scan result metadata (issue descriptions, element selectors, screenshots) — never your source code. All data is encrypted at rest (AES-256) and in transit (TLS 1.3). Enterprise customers can choose EU-only data residency or deploy fully on-premise.

We offer a 30-day money-back guarantee on all paid plans. If you're not satisfied for any reason within 30 days of your first payment, contact billing@luminarystudio.io and we'll refund you in full, no questions asked.

No automated tool can replace manual testing and user research with disabled people. Luminary is designed to catch the ~40% of WCAG issues that can be detected automatically, freeing your team to focus manual effort on the more nuanced cognitive, motor, and contextual accessibility considerations that require human judgement. We include a manual test checklist for each scan to guide the remaining work.
